items.evidence: apps/web/src/components/PlaidConnectFlow.tsx:92-94; apps/api/src/plaid/takeover.ts:65-78; apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:85-121
items.title: Drive the Plaid takeover archive boundary from Plaid's real earliest_available_date so post-takeover history cannot double-count
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-10): PlaidConnectFlow.tsx:92-94 hard-codes the takeover window to exactly 90 days back, with an in-code comment conceding "Phase 2 should ask Plaid for the real earliest_available_date". commitTakeover (plaid/takeover.ts:65-78) therefore archives CSV rows only WHERE posted_date >= that date, one-shot and never re-run, while Plaid backfills up to 24 months of the same history as source='plaid' (plaid/sync.ts:85-121 re-archives nothing). Neither unique index catches the overlap: CSV raw_description differs from the Plaid name, and CSV rows carry a NULL plaid_transaction_id. Every real transaction older than 90 days therefore appears twice in transactions_live and double-counts in all analytics. Fix: drive the archive boundary from Plaid's real earliest_available_date per item, or re-archive overlapping live CSV rows on each sync as older Plaid history backfills. Add a regression test covering a takeover with CSV rows predating the window. Live exposure is limited today because Plaid is Sandbox-only (D-012), but this bites the moment Production is approved. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

items.evidence: scripts/push-to-github.ps1:74-82; scripts/git-guard.ps1:55
items.title: Capture $pushExit immediately after git push so Confirm-GitIntact cannot mask a rejected push
items.prompt: YaB MED (bar-raise 2026-07-10): push-to-github.ps1:74-82 runs `git push`, then `Confirm-GitIntact`, then tests `$LASTEXITCODE`. Confirm-GitIntact (git-guard.ps1:55) itself runs `git rev-parse --verify HEAD`, which overwrites $LASTEXITCODE with its own exit 0. A rejected push therefore reports success, and release.ps1 proceeds to tag and post to Discord for a version that never reached origin. Fix: capture `$pushExit = $LASTEXITCODE` on the line immediately after `git push`, then call Confirm-GitIntact, then test `$pushExit`. While in this file, also delete the blind `Remove-Item -Force .git\index.lock` block at lines 28-32 (see bar-raise-2026-07-04-yab-push-blind-lock-delete); it runs ahead of the Assert-GitSafe call at line 56 that exists to handle exactly that race. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

items.title: Gnosis handler says 'keep the answer inline' - shipped model is deletable KV answer cards + ^q qids
items.detail: X:\YesAndGnosis\CLAUDE.md:37 reads '[x] = answered (keep the answer inline)'. That is the exact inverse of the shipped model: web/build.mjs:1676-1695 strips inline answers out of question text into deletable KV answer cards ('Answers live in the answer store ... never smushed into the question text'), and each question ends with a stable ^qXXXXXXXX block-ref token that IS the qid (build.mjs:1661-1684; worker.js:168 validates ^[0-9a-f]{8}$). The token must never be stripped. This convention is what fixed 30 orphaned/truncated answers in v0.3.33; the handler as written would reintroduce that exact bug. Fix: replace line 37 with the correct rule and add the never-strip-the-qid note.
items.completionNote: 2026-07-11 queue-drain-4h: DRAINED. Rewrote X:\YesAndGnosis\CLAUDE.md line 37 answered-question rule. Verified against shipped code first: web/build.mjs:2402-2411 strips the inline DM answer tail out of the displayed question into deletable KV answer cards ("Answers live in the answer store ... never smushed into the question text"), and the trailing ^q[0-9a-f]{8} block-ref token IS the qid (worker.js:358 validates /^[0-9a-f]{8}$/). So the old '[x] = answered (keep the answer inline)' was the exact inverse of reality. Reframe both docs so auth + RLS + single-cat agent sharing read as LIVE, and only the FULL merged co-ownership / email invites / reg-number join + certificate verification model reads as pending. Align wording to the AUTH-OWNERSHIP-DESIGN.md status header. NOTE: the two pure-factual sub-fixes ('0001-0006' -> '0001-0007' and 'Auth magic links' -> 'Auth email OTP codes') were ALREADY auto-applied to PROJECT_SPEC.md this run; do not redo them, just fold them into the surrounding reframe. Optional low-priority add-on (audit Fix #4): add a Turnstile/CAPTCHA login clause to the PROJECT_SPEC.md Security bullet (live at v0.17.12; /api/config serves the site key, login renders the widget, CSP opens challenges.cloudflare.com) or leave it to docs/SECURITY.md. Structural because it edits CLAUDE.md (handler files are never auto-edited by drift-auto-fix) and rephrases a canonical product-state claim across two docs; needs a deliberate session. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Ships via X:\YesAndRing\scripts\release.ps1. EXPANDED 2026-07-10 by audit-ring-nightly (CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-07-10.md). The same doc region carries three further stale claims; fix them in the SAME deliberate pass. (a) HIGHEST PRIORITY, new + actively misleading: CLAUDE.md line 25 states the /api/state endpoint 'is unauthenticated (closed-beta grade; cat results are public TICA data)'. That is FALSE. src/index.js handleStateGet (~:770) and handleStatePost (~:795) both call verifyUser(request, env) and return 401 unauthorized when it fails; verifyUser (~:725) requires an Authorization: Bearer token and validates it against Supabase /auth/v1/user. POST keys the blob by the VERIFIED identity so no client can write another user's state; GET enforces self-only reads with a 403 unless the caller is a super-admin performing an audited view-as. Rewrite that line to describe the real posture. Risk if left: a contributor trusts the doc and strips the verifyUser/403 checks as dead closed-beta scaffolding. (b) CLAUDE.md line 20 and PROJECT_SPEC.md lines 74-75 both say persistence is 'localStorage until the backend lands'. The backend landed: schedulePush mirrors LOCAL to POST /api/state, boot hydrates from GET /api/state. localStorage is now a cache in front of a server of record, not the system of record. (c) CLAUDE.md lines 24-25 describe state as KV-only (KV RATINGS, key u:<email>). Reality: handleStatePost dual-writes KV AND pgStateWrite (RLS Postgres); handleStateGet reads Postgres when env.STATE_READ == 'pg', with KV fallback + lazy backfill, and KV remains the view-as/sharing source and rollback path. The STATE_READ migration flag is documented nowhere in the canonical layer. Also note for the reframe: cat sharing is LIVE, not pending - /api/share/grant, /api/share/revoke, /api/share/by-me and /api/shared-cats are all routed and implemented. And PROJECT_SPEC.md now self-contradicts within three lines: line 24 already says 'Auth email OTP codes' while line 26 still says 'magic-link auth ... not yet the live path'.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [HIGH, bar-raise finding auth-01]: the Cloudflare Worker verifies no Supabase JWT and trusts a client-supplied body.userId for every entitlement decision, so anyone who knows an entitled account's UUID can spoof Pro access to the paid AI-spend endpoints. Evidence: worker/src/lib.js:1717-1734 (_requireEntitledUser) and :1678-1701 (_checkSubscriptionEntitled) read body.userId with only a shape check; worker/src/handlers/billing.js:53,446,566,617 and contribute.js:290-297,365-372,403-410 do the same. Fix: require the Supabase access-token in an Authorization header on the billing, AI-spend, and contribution routes, verify it (Supabase /auth/v1/user or JWT signature), and derive userId from the verified claim instead of the request body. Live paid product, so treat as pre-1.0. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1; app.js is build output, edit src/ + worker/src/. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding finance-03]: checkout creation neither blocks an already-active subscriber nor sends a Stripe idempotency key, so a double-tap or a re-opened upgrade modal can create a second live subscription on the same customer and double-bill the user. Evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:566-604 (handleBillingCheckout) has no pre-check of subscription_status and no Idempotency-Key; src/subscription.ts:158 openUpgradeModal() is window-exposed with no entitled-guard. Fix: before creating a Checkout Session, load the profile and short-circuit if subscription_status is active/trialing, and pass an Idempotency-Key keyed by userId+plan on the /checkout/sessions call. Live payments, so pre-1.0. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding sync-01]: bags, discs, and collection use replace-on-load plus blind delete-missing upsert (last-write-wins, no timestamp guard), unlike rounds which union-merge with tombstones, so a disc added on device A while device B edits offline can be deleted when B syncs. Evidence: src/cloud.ts:673 (cloudLoadBagsAndDiscs wholesale-replaces state.bags), :855-883 (cloudSyncBagsAndDiscs full-set upsert + delete-missing, no guard) vs :1098-1112 (roundHistory id-keyed union merge). Fix: give bags/discs/collection the same id-keyed union-merge-with-tombstones treatment rounds already use, or add an updatedAt guard so a stale device cannot delete rows it never loaded. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

items.notes: 2026-07-10 loop-tick-hourly (R5 drain): DRAINED to completion. Both halves resolved. Not a guard, not a watchdog, not the live node server or cloudflared. Windows-side writers (the Edit/Write tools, or .NET File IO from PowerShell) do persist. Verified by writing from PowerShell and reading the new bytes back from an independent bash process. (2) The typo is fixed: scripts/release.ps1 line 6 now reads '.\\scripts\\release.ps1'. Applied as a byte-preserving in-place [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText (File::Replace is unsupported on this mount); the file's CRLF endings and all other bytes are untouched, 1736 -> 1737 bytes, and 'git -c core.autocrlf=false diff' confirms exactly 1 insertion / 1 deletion. The repo has core.autocrlf=true, so a plain 'git diff --stat' misleadingly renders 31/31; that is display noise, not a rewrite. Comment-only, no behavior change. items.title: Generate or guard sitemap.xml so new project cards cannot silently miss it
items.detail: sitemap.xml is hand-maintained. Nothing under scripts/ or .github/ writes it, and the site standard does not name it as a step when a project card lands. Result: projects/cattery/, projects/gnosis/ and projects/ring/ all shipped homepage cards in c7231e8 and never reached the sitemap, so three of the newest public projects were invisible to any crawler trusting it. The missing URLs were added by hand in the 2026-07-10 drift-fix pass, which fixes the symptom, not the cause. The durable fix is one of two. Either extend scripts/update-project-pages.mjs to emit sitemap.xml from the same status/data/*.json plus projects/*/ sources it already walks, which is preferred because it runs in the Pages deploy workflow and so self-heals on every release. Or add a release.ps1 integrity guard, alongside the existing index.html and CNAME guards, that asserts every projects/*/index.html except the here-there-be-hordes redirect stub has a matching <loc> entry, and aborts if not. Design pages stay out of the sitemap on purpose because they are client-gated. Not auto-fixed because it adds a build step or a release guard, which is a structural call. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

items.title: Rising has 32 untracked .uid files while 115 are tracked
items.detail: BrackishRising tracks 115 Godot .uid files but leaves 32 untracked, and .gitignore does not exclude them. The untracked ones all sit next to modules created during the god-file decomposition (unit_commander.gd.uid, grid_renderer.gd.uid, unit_collision.gd.uid and the rest). Godot generates a .uid per script and expects it committed alongside; a missing one makes the editor regenerate a fresh uid, which breaks any scene or resource referencing the old one. Pick one rule and apply it to all 147: commit the 32, or gitignore every .uid and drop the 115 from the index. Committing them matches current practice and Godot's own guidance. Low urgency because a solo dev with one checkout will not notice, but it bites the moment a second clone exists.

items.title: photos storage bucket is enumerable by any client; exposes pending (unscreened) catteries' photos
items.detail: X:\YesAndCattery\supabase\migrations�6_photos_storage_bucket.sql declares `create policy "photos public read" on storage.objects for select using (bucket_id = 'photos')`. A select policy on storage.objects governs LISTING, not just object fetches. The bucket is already public, so objects serve by URL without any policy - this policy buys nothing for display but lets any client call the storage list API and enumerate every object. Supabase security advisor flags it as public_bucket_allows_listing (WARN). Impact is project-specific: a cattery is deliberately not public until screened, yet photos are uploaded during the pending application, so enumeration exposes unscreened catteries' photos that RLS otherwise hides. Fix: drop or scope the broad select policy (e.g. restrict to owner + screened-cattery paths), confirm app.js display paths use direct public object URLs and never the list API, then re-run get_advisors(security) to confirm the lint clears. Note this policy also lives only in a repo file that was never applied through prod migration history (see canonical-audit-2026-07-02-cattery-missing-migrations) - the live bucket policy must be changed in prod too, not just in the file.

items.title: SECURITY-ASSESSMENT marks rate limiting "fixed" but srv_* RPCs bypass the Worker limiter entirely
items.detail: docs/SECURITY-ASSESSMENT-2026-07-01.md finding #3 ("No rate limiting or CAPTCHA on public write paths") is marked fixed, remediated by "Cloudflare Workers rate-limiting binding on all /api POSTs". But src/index.js:72 authenticates to Supabase with the ANON key and passes p_secret: env.WORKER_SECRET, so every srv_* function is anon-executable and reachable directly at ${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/rpc/srv_mark_released using the publishable anon key that ships in wrangler.jsonc and public/config.js. That path never traverses the Worker, so the Worker rate limiter is not a control on the money-path RPCs. This is intentional and documented (0015 header comment) and srv_ok() compares a SHA-256 of the secret, so there is NO breach and no schema change wanted. The defect is documentation accuracy: the sole control on srv_mark_paid/released/refunded is WORKER_SECRET entropy, and the assessment implies a rate-limit coverage it does not have. Fix: amend finding #3 to scope the remediation to the Worker /api surface, and add an explicit accepted-risk entry for the PostgREST srv_* surface naming WORKER_SECRET as its control. Optionally consider whether the Worker should use a service_role key so srv_* EXECUTE can be revoked from anon/authenticated as real defense in depth.

items.title: wrangler.jsonc declares SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID as an empty vars entry while README + code say to set it as a secret
items.detail: wrangler.jsonc vars contains "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID": "" (empty plain-text binding), but README.md:61 and the /api/setup help string at src/index.ts:141 both instruct `npx wrangler secret put SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID`. (README.md:64 offers pasting into vars as an alternative.) Plain-text vars and secrets share one binding namespace, so an empty var declared in config is at best redundant and at worst shadows/overwrites a same-named secret on `wrangler deploy` -- which scripts/release.ps1 runs on every ship. If the frame ID resolves empty in production, src/skylight.ts:64-67 throws "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID is not set", breaking lists/chores/tasks (the reverse-API surfaces). Events are unaffected: under CALENDAR_DRIVER="google" (D1) they route through google.ts, which never reads the frame ID -- that asymmetry is likely why this went unnoticed. NOT VERIFIED LIVE: the Worker is private and the nightly routine holds no credentials, so it is unconfirmed whether a secret is currently set and whether this wrangler version actually clobbers it. Next step: run `npx wrangler secret list` (or GET /api/setup with the PIN) to confirm. Then either (a) drop the empty SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID line from wrangler.jsonc vars and keep it a secret, or (b) paste the real ID into vars and drop the `secret put` instructions from README.md:61 + src/index.ts:141. Pick one and make docs+config agree. Not auto-fixed: deploy-affecting config on a repo a parallel session co-owns.

items.evidence: src/dates.ts:33-37 epochOf anchors naive values to UTC but parses absolute forms as true instants; src/router.ts:28 compares them
items.prompt: In Skylight, start/end ordering is sound when both endpoints share a format, but a mixed naive-versus-absolute pair (reachable from the JSON API or the LLM email parser) is compared across two time frames and is off by the real UTC offset, so it can be wrongly rejected or wrongly accepted. Normalise both endpoints through DEFAULT_TIMEZONE before comparison, or reject a mixed pair outright. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.title: Add a debug_flags-gated frame-time telemetry logger for the enemy field
items.detail: MED, bar-raise 2026-07-11. A slow frame-time regression on the perf-critical enemy field (the hundreds-to-thousands-enemy target) has no automatic signal: grep of source/ shows no Performance.get_monitor / frame-time sink and docs/OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md relies on a manual screenshot-the-profiler loop, which conflicts with the efficiency-first hard rule's need to catch creep early. Add a debug_flags-gated per-run logger that samples frame time vs alive enemy count (gate via source/autoloads/debug_flags.gd; alive count from the maintained _alive counter in source/world3d/enemy_field_3d.gd) writing to the log or a CSV on a hotkey, zero-cost when the flag is off. HBH source change: trips the GDD-update+release hard rule, so attended session only, ship via X:\HereBeHordes\scriptselease.ps1 with a pill bump, changelog entry, and an OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md row. FUSE-truncation-sensitive: Python atomic write with read-back for any autoload/entry-point edit.
